sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the ASCII layout more carefully:
Row 35-38: BBBBYYYYYYYYYYYYYOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOB (wide yellow/orange corridor)
Row 39-43: BBBBBBBBBYYYYYYYYOOOOOOOOOOOOBBBBBB (narrower corridor)
Row 44-48: BBBBBBBBBSSSSSYYYOOSSSSSBBBBBBBBBBB (blues in chambers)
Row 49-53: BBBBBBBBBBBBBBYYYOOBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB (narrow passage)
Row 54-58: BBBBBBBBBBBBBBYYYOOBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBO (narrow passage)
Row 59: YYYYYYYYYYYYYYYYYOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO (wide corridor at bottom)
So the structure is:
- Top: wide corridor (rows 35-38)
- Middle: narrower corridor (rows 39-43)
- Blues in chambers (rows 44-48)
- Bottom: narrow passage (rows 49-58)
- Very bottom: wide corridor (row 59)
The blues are in chambers at rows 44-48. The left blue is at cols 24-28 and the right blue is at cols 34-38. Between them is "YYYOO" (cols 29-33).
Looking at the structure, the blues can move:
- Up: into the corridor at rows 39-43
- Down: into the passage at rows 49-53
- Left/Right: within the chamber
The left blue is in a chamber bounded by:
- Left: black (B)
- Right: yellow (Y) at cols 29-31
- Top: black (B) at rows 39-43
- Bottom: black (B) at rows 49-53
Wait, looking at row 44: BBBBBBBBBSSSSSYYYOOSSSSSBBBBBBBBBBB
The left blue (SSSSS) is at positions 9-13 (relative to the crop starting at col 15, so cols 24-28).
To the right of the left blue is "YYY" (cols 29-31), then "OO" (cols 32-33), then the right blue "SSSSS" (cols 34-38).
So the blues are separated by "YYYOO" (5 columns). The left blue can move right into the yellow cells, and the right blue can move left into the orange cells. But they're in separate chambers.
Actually, I think I need to reconsider. The blues are 5x5 squares. They can move up/down/left/right as long as there's a 5x5 space available. The chambers are:
- Left chamber: rows 44-48, cols 24-28 (5x5 space for the blue)
- Right chamber: rows 44-48, cols 34-38 (5x5 space for the blue)
The blues can move within these chambers. The left blue can move up into the corridor at rows 39-43, and the right blue can also move up.
Looking at the structure, I think the goal might be to get both blues to meet at the same position. But they're in separate chambers separated by the yellow/orange gap.
Wait, let me look at the initial positions again:
- Initial left blue: rows 49-53, cols 19-23
- Initial right blue: rows 49-53, cols 39-43
- Current left blue: rows 44-48, cols 24-28
- Current right blue: rows 44-48, cols 34-38
The blues moved from rows 49-53 to rows 44-48 (up 5 rows). The left blue moved from cols 19-23 to cols 24-28 (right 5 cols). The right blue moved from cols 39-43 to cols 34-38 (left 5 cols).
So the blues have been moving toward the center. The goal might be to get them to meet at the center. Currently they're at rows 44-48, with the left blue at cols 24-28 and the right blue at cols 34-38. The gap between them is cols 29-33 (5 columns).
If the blues continue moving toward each other, they'll eventually meet. But the left blue is blocked by the yellow cells at cols 29-31, and the right blue is blocked by the orange cells at cols 32-33.
Actually, I think the blues CAN move through the yellow/orange cells. The yellow/orange cells are part of the corridor, not walls. The black cells are the walls.
Let me try RIGHT again to see if the left blue can move further right.
